title: Shujing: Canon of Yao
source: Old Text and New Text recensions
description: From the Shujing (Book of Documents) — the foundational Chinese historical text, recording the speeches of legendary sage-kings.
introduction: From the Shujing (Book of Documents) — the foundational Chinese historical text, recording the speeches of legendary sage-kings.

translation.text: Examining into antiquity, we find that the Emperor Yao was called Fang-xun. He was reverential, intelligent, accomplished, and thoughtful — naturally and without effort. He was sincerely courteous, and capable of all complaisance. The bright influence of these qualities was felt through the four quarters of the land.
